text: If the roots of \(x^3-ax^2+bx-c=0\) are in GP, then \(\dfrac{b^3}{a^3} =\)
explanation: Let roots be \(\dfrac{p}{r},\ p,\ pr\). Product of roots: \(p^3=c\), so \(p=c^{1/3}\). Sum of roots: \(p\left(\dfrac{1}{r}+1+r\right)=a\). Sum of products in pairs: \(p^2\left(\dfrac{1}{r}+r+1\right)=b\). Dividing: \(\dfrac{b}{a}=p=\dfrac{b^{1/3}}{a^{1/3}}\cdot\ldots\) directly, \(\dfrac{b}{a}=p \Rightarrow p=\dfrac{b}{a}\). Then \(p^3=c \Rightarrow \dfrac{b^3}{a^3}=c\).
name: Roots in GP Condition
subject: maths

text: The energy of an electron in a hydrogen-like ion orbit with a radius of 52.9 pm is: (ground state energy of hydrogen atom = \(-2.18 \times 10^{-18}\) J)
explanation:
Bohr's radius formula: \(r_n = r_0 \times \frac{n^2}{Z}\)

Given \(r_n = 52.9\) pm = \(r_0 \times \frac{n^2}{Z}\), so \(\frac{n^2}{Z} = 1\), meaning \(n^2 = Z\).

Energy: \(E_n = E_0 \times \frac{Z^2}{n^2} = E_0 \times \frac{n^4}{n^2} = E_0 \times n^2\)

For \(n = 2\): \(E = -2.18 \times 10^{-18} \times 4 = -8.72 \times 10^{-18}\) J
name: Bohr's Model вЂ” Energy from Orbit Radius
subject: che

text: If \(x=\dfrac{3}{10}+\dfrac{3\cdot7}{10\cdot15}+\dfrac{3\cdot7\cdot9}{10\cdot15\cdot20}+\cdots\), then \(5x+8 =\)
explanation: Rewriting with \(3\cdot5\) in numerator: \(x=\dfrac{3\cdot5}{5\cdot10}+\cdots\). General term matches \((1+y)^n\) with \(n=-\tfrac{3}{2}\) and \(y=-\tfrac{2}{5}\). So \(x+1=(1-\tfrac{2}{5})^{-3/2}=\left(\tfrac{3}{5}\right)^{-3/2}=\left(\tfrac{5}{3}\right)^{3/2}\). Then \(5x+8=5\left[\left(\tfrac{5}{3}\right)^{3/2}-1\right]+8=5\left(\tfrac{5}{3}\right)^{3/2}+3=\dfrac{5^{5/2}}{3^{3/2}}+3=\dfrac{25\sqrt5}{3\sqrt3}+3\). PDF answer: \(\dfrac{25\sqrt5}{3\sqrt3}\).

text: 6 g of graphite is burnt in a bomb calorimeter at 25В°C. The temperature of water increased from 25В°C to 31В°C. If \(\Delta H\) of this reaction is \(-248\) kJ molвЃ»В№, find \(C_V\) (in kJ KвЃ»В№) of the bomb calorimeter.
explanation:
In a bomb calorimeter, \(W = 0\) (constant volume), so \(\Delta H \approx \Delta U = q\).

For 12 g of graphite (1 mol): \(\Delta H = -248\) kJ

For 6 g (0.5 mol):
\[q = \frac{248 \times 6}{12} = 124 \text{ kJ}\]

Using \(q = C_V \Delta T\) with \(\Delta T = 31 - 25 = 6\) K:
\[C_V = \frac{124}{6} \approx 20.67 \text{ kJ K}^{-1}\]
name: Heat Capacity of Bomb Calorimeter
subject: che

text: Which among the following is a unitless physical quantity?
explanation:
Mole fraction is defined as the ratio of moles of one component to the total moles of all components in a solution:
\[x_A = \frac{n_A}{n_A + n_B}\]
Since it is a ratio of moles to moles, it is dimensionless (unitless). Also, the sum of mole fractions of all components always equals 1. Molality has units of mol/kg, and molarity and normality have units of mol/L.

\[n_{\text{CO}_2} = \chi \times n_{\text{H}_2\text{O}} = 10^{-3} \times 55.5 = 0.0555 \text{ mol}\]

Mass = \(0.0555 \times 44 = 2.44\) g/L

text: If \(f: \mathbb{R}-\left\{\tfrac{3}{7}\right\} \to \mathbb{R}-\left\{\tfrac{3}{7}\right\}\) is given by \(f(x)=\dfrac{3x+5}{7x-3}\), which of the following is NOT true?
explanation: Find \(f^{-1}\): set \(y=\dfrac{3x+5}{7x-3}\), solve for \(x\): \(x=\dfrac{3y+5}{7y-3}=f(y)\). So \(f^{-1}=f\) (involutory). Therefore \(f\circ f = \text{id}\), so \((f\circ f)(x)=x\) вњ“. Also \((f\circ f\circ f\circ f)(x)=x\) вњ“. But \((f\circ f\circ f)(x)=f(x)\neq x\) in general вЂ” so (c) is NOT true.

text: If \(\alpha, \beta\) are roots of \(x^2+bx+c=0\) and \(\gamma, \delta\) are roots of \(x^2+b_1x+c_1=0\), and \(\gamma < \alpha < \delta < \beta\), then \((c-c_1)^2 <\)

text: In \(\triangle ABC\), \(\dfrac{1}{r_1^2} + \dfrac{1}{r_2^2} + \dfrac{1}{r_3^2} + \dfrac{1}{r^2} =\)
explanation:
Using \(r_1 = \dfrac{\Delta}{s-a}\), \(r_2 = \dfrac{\Delta}{s-b}\), \(r_3 = \dfrac{\Delta}{s-c}\), \(r = \dfrac{\Delta}{s}\):

\[\sum\frac{1}{r_1^2} + \frac{1}{r^2} = \frac{(s-a)^2+(s-b)^2+(s-c)^2+s^2}{\Delta^2}\]

\[= \frac{4s^2 + a^2+b^2+c^2 - 2s(a+b+c)}{\Delta^2} = \frac{4s^2+a^2+b^2+c^2-4s^2}{\Delta^2} = \frac{a^2+b^2+c^2}{\Delta^2}\]

text: In \(\triangle ABC\) with \(\angle C = 60В°\), if \(a\cos^2\dfrac{C}{2} + c\cos^2\dfrac{A}{2} = \dfrac{3b}{2}\), then
explanation:
Using \(\cos^2\dfrac{\theta}{2} = \dfrac{s(s-\theta\text{-opp})}{\text{adj sides product}}\):

\[a\cdot\frac{s(s-c)}{ab} + c\cdot\frac{s(s-a)}{bc} = \frac{3b}{2}\]

\[\frac{s(s-c)}{b} + \frac{s(s-a)}{b} = \frac{3b}{2}\]

\[\frac{s}{b}(2s-a-c) = \frac{3b}{2}\]

\[\frac{s\cdot b}{b} = \frac{3b}{2} \implies s = \frac{3b}{2}\]

\[\frac{a+b+c}{2} = \frac{3b}{2} \implies a+b+c = 3b \implies a+c = 2b \implies 2b = a+c\]

text: If \(\theta \in \left(0, \dfrac{\pi}{2}\right)\) satisfies \(\cos^2\theta \cdot \sec^4\theta + \sec^2\theta = 0\), then \(\sin^2\theta =\)
explanation:
Given \(\cos 2\theta \cdot \sec^4\theta + \sec^2\theta = 0\) with \(\theta \in \left(0,\dfrac{\pi}{2}\right)\).

Using \(\cos 2\theta = 1 - 2\sin^2\theta\) and factoring \(\sec^2\theta\):

\[\sec^2\theta[(1-2\sin^2\theta)\sec^2\theta + 1] = 0\]

Since \(\sec^2\theta \neq 0\):

\[(1-\tan^2\theta)\sec^2\theta + \sec^2\theta = 0\]

Wait вЂ” rewriting with \(\cos 2\theta = 1 - \tan^2\theta / \sec^2\theta\):

\[\sec^2\theta[1 - \tan^2\theta + 1] = 0 \implies \tan^2\theta = 2\]

\[\sin^2\theta = \frac{\tan^2\theta}{1+\tan^2\theta} = \frac{2}{3}\]
name: Sec and Cos2 Theta Equation

text: If \(\csc\theta + \cot\theta = \dfrac{1}{3}\), then \(\theta\) lies in the
explanation:
Given \(\csc\theta + \cot\theta = \dfrac{1}{3}\) вЂ¦ (i)

Using \(\csc^2\theta - \cot^2\theta = 1\), we get \(\csc\theta - \cot\theta = 3\) вЂ¦ (ii)

Adding: \(2\csc\theta = \dfrac{10}{3}\), so \(\csc\theta = \dfrac{5}{3} > 0\) (\(\sin\theta > 0\)).

Subtracting (i) from (ii): \(2\cot\theta = 3 - \dfrac{1}{3} = \dfrac{8}{3}\), so \(\cot\theta = \dfrac{-4}{3} < 0\).

\(\sin\theta > 0\) and \(\cot\theta < 0\) (i.e., \(\cos\theta < 0\)) в†’ \(\theta\) is in the **second quadrant**.

text: The equation of line through the intersection of \(3x-4y+1=0\) and \(5x+y-1=0\) which cuts equal intercepts on the axes is

\[(3+5\lambda)x + (\lambda-4)y + (1-\lambda) = 0\]

For equal intercepts, the \(x\)- and \(y\)-intercepts must be equal:

\[\frac{\lambda-1}{3+5\lambda} = \frac{\lambda-1}{\lambda-4} \implies \lambda-4 = 5\lambda+3 \implies \lambda=-\frac{7}{4}\] (for \(\lambda \neq 1\))

Substituting: \(3-\dfrac{35}{4} = -\dfrac{23}{4}\) etc., giving \(23x+23y-11=0\).
name: Line with Equal Intercepts

explanation: Multiply: \(8=(Ax+B)(x-2)+C(x+3)^2\). Put \(x=2\): \(8=25C \Rightarrow C=\tfrac{8}{25}\). Put \(x=0\): \(8=-2B+9C=-2B+\tfrac{72}{25} \Rightarrow B=-\tfrac{64}{25}\). Put \(x=1\): \(8=-(A+B)+16C \Rightarrow A=-\tfrac{8}{25}\). Now \(25(B+8C-A)=25\left(-\tfrac{64}{25}+\tfrac{64}{25}+\tfrac{8}{25}\right)=25\cdot\tfrac{8}{25}=8\).

text: A solution is prepared by dissolving 10 g of a non-volatile solute in 360 g of water. The relative lowering of vapour pressure is \(5 \times 10^{-3}\). The molar mass of the solute in g molвЃ»В№ is:
explanation:
\(n_A = \frac{360}{18} = 20\) mol (water), \(n_B = \frac{10}{M_B}\)

\[\frac{\Delta p}{p^\circ} = \chi_B = \frac{n_B}{n_A + n_B}\]

\[5 \times 10^{-3} = \frac{10/M_B}{20 + 10/M_B}\]

Solving:
\[5 \times 10^{-3}(20M_B + 10) = 10\]
\[0.1M_B + 0.05 = 10 \Rightarrow M_B = \frac{9.95}{0.1} \approx 99.5 \text{ g/mol}\]

\[\text{SO}_2 + \text{H}_2\text{O} \rightarrow \text{H}_2\text{SO}_3 \xrightarrow{\text{oxidation}} \text{H}_2\text{SO}_4\]

**Nitrogen dioxide (NOв‚‚):**
\[3\text{NO}_2 + \text{H}_2\text{O} \rightarrow 2\text{HNO}_3 + \text{NO}\]

The resulting **sulphuric acid and nitric acid** dissolve in rainwater, lowering its pH below 5.6, forming acid rain.

Sources: SOв‚‚ from burning fossil fuels and volcanic activity; NOв‚‚ from vehicle exhausts and power plants.

- CFC causes ozone depletion, not acid rain

text: The sum of the values of \(x\) satisfying \(\sin^{-1}\dfrac{3x}{5} + \sin^{-1}\dfrac{4x}{5} = \sin^{-1}x\) is
explanation:
Using the formula \(\sin^{-1}A + \sin^{-1}B = \sin^{-1}(A\sqrt{1-B^2}+B\sqrt{1-A^2})\):

\[\sin^{-1}\!\left(\frac{3x}{5}\sqrt{1-\frac{16x^2}{25}} + \frac{4x}{5}\sqrt{1-\frac{9x^2}{25}}\right) = \sin^{-1}x\]

Either \(x = 0\) is a solution, or squaring the inner equation:

\[\frac{9(25-16x^2)}{625} + \frac{16(25-9x^2)}{625} + \frac{24}{625}\sqrt{(25-16x^2)(25-9x^2)} = x^2\]

Simplification gives \(24\sqrt{(25-16x^2)(25-9x^2)} = 288x^2\), leading to:

\[(25-16x^2)(25-9x^2) = 144x^4 \implies 625 - 625x^2 = 0 \implies x = \pm 1\]

So the solutions are \(x = 0, 1, -1\). Their sum: \(0 + 1 + (-1) = 0\).
name: Sum of Solutions of Arcsin Equation

text: If the standard deviation of the numbers \(2, 3, 2x\) and \(11\) is \(3.5\), find the possible values of \(x\).

Using \(\sigma^2=\dfrac{1}{n}\sum(x_i-\bar{x})^2\): \((3.5)^2=\dfrac{1}{4}\left[\left(-2-\tfrac{x}{2}\right)^2+\left(-1-\tfrac{x}{2}\right)^2+\left(\tfrac{3x}{2}-4\right)^2+\left(7-\tfrac{x}{2}\right)^2\right]\). This simplifies to \(3x^2-16x+21=0\Rightarrow(3x-7)(x-3)=0\Rightarrow x=\dfrac{7}{3}\) or \(x=3\).

text:
In which of the following reactions does \(\text{H}_2\text{O}_2\) act as an oxidising agent?

(i) \(2\text{Fe}^{2+} + \text{H}_2\text{O}_2 \rightarrow\)
(ii) \(2\text{MnO}_4^- + 6\text{H}^+ + 5\text{H}_2\text{O}_2 \rightarrow\)
(iii) \(\text{I}_2 + \text{H}_2\text{O}_2 + 2\text{OH}^- \rightarrow\)
(iv) \(\text{Mn}^{2+} + \text{H}_2\text{O}_2 \rightarrow\)
explanation: An oxidising agent accepts electrons (gets reduced) from the other species. In reaction (i), \(\text{Fe}^{2+}\) is oxidised to \(\text{Fe}^{3+}\), meaning \(\text{H}_2\text{O}_2\) itself gets reduced to \(\text{H}_2\text{O}\) вЂ” so it acts as an oxidising agent. In reaction (iv), \(\text{Mn}^{2+}\) is oxidised to \(\text{Mn}^{4+}\), again with \(\text{H}_2\text{O}_2\) getting reduced вЂ” so it acts as an oxidising agent here too. In reactions (ii) and (iii), \(\text{H}_2\text{O}_2\) is itself oxidised (loses electrons), so it acts as a reducing agent there. Therefore, reactions (i) and (iv) are the ones where \(\text{H}_2\text{O}_2\) is the oxidising agent.
name: Hв‚‚Oв‚‚ as Oxidising Agent
subject: che

text: Let the complex numbers \(\alpha\) and \(\dfrac{1}{\alpha}\) lie on circles \((x-x_0)^2+(y-y_0)^2 = r^2\) and \((x-x_0)^2+(y-y_0)^2 = 4r^2\) respectively. If \(z_0 = x_0 + iy_0\) satisfies \(2|z_0|^2 = r^2 + 2\), then \(|\alpha| =\)
explanation: Since \(\alpha\) lies on the circle of radius \(r\) centred at \(z_0\): \(|\alpha - z_0|^2 = r^2\), expanding gives \(|\alpha|^2 + |z_0|^2 - (\alpha\bar{z_0} + \bar{\alpha}z_0) = r^2\) ...(i). Since \(\tfrac{1}{\alpha}\) lies on the circle of radius \(2r\): \(\left|\tfrac{1}{\alpha} - z_0\right|^2 = 4r^2\), which gives \(1 + |z_0|^2|\alpha|^2 - (\alpha\bar{z_0} + \bar{\alpha}z_0) = 4r^2|\alpha|^2\) ...(ii). Subtracting (i) from (ii): \((|\alpha|^2 - 1)(|z_0|^2 - 1) = r^2(4|\alpha|^2 - 1)\). Substituting \(|z_0|^2 = \dfrac{r^2+2}{2}\): \((|\alpha|^2-1)\cdot\dfrac{r^2}{2} = r^2(4|\alpha|^2-1)\), which simplifies to \(7|\alpha|^2 = 1\), so \(|\alpha| = \dfrac{1}{\sqrt{7}}\).
name: Complex Numbers on Circles
subject: maths

text: \([4\mathbf{a}+3\mathbf{b}-\mathbf{c},\;4\mathbf{a}+3\mathbf{b}+2\mathbf{c},\;\mathbf{a}-4\mathbf{b}-\mathbf{c}]=(\lambda^2+\lambda+1)[\mathbf{a}\;\mathbf{b}\;\mathbf{c}]\). The values of \(\lambda\) are

text:
Assertion (A): \(\text{CuI}_2\) cannot be prepared by the reaction of \(\text{Cu}^{2+}(aq)\) with \(\text{I}^-(aq)\).

Reason (R): Aqueous \(\text{Cu}^{2+}\) solution is blue in colour.

The correct answer is
options: Both (A) and (R) are correct and (R) is the correct explanation of (A)
Both (A) and (R) are correct but (R) is not the correct explanation of (A)
(A) is correct but (R) is not correct
(A) is not correct but (R) is correct
explanation:
**Assertion (A) is correct:** When \(\text{Cu}^{2+}\) reacts with \(\text{I}^-\), instead of forming \(\text{CuI}_2\), the reaction produces \(\text{Cu}_2\text{I}_2\) (copper(I) iodide) and \(\text{I}_2\). This is because the overall cell potential for \(\text{CuI}_2\) formation is **negative** (non-spontaneous). The two half-reactions give: \(E_{\text{cell}} = 0.159 + (-0.535) = -0.376\text{ V} < 0\), confirming \(\text{CuI}_2\) cannot form.

**Reason (R) is also correct:** Aqueous \(\text{Cu}^{2+}\) solution is indeed blue (due to the \([\text{Cu(H}_2\text{O)}_4]^{2+}\) complex).

However, R does **not explain** A вЂ” the blue colour has nothing to do with why \(\text{CuI}_2\) cannot be prepared. Therefore: **Both A and R are correct but R is not the correct explanation of A**.
name: Questions Item 23

\[y=-\frac{4}{3}x+\frac{4}{-4/3}=-\frac{4}{3}x-3\]

text: If \(\tan A - \tan B = x\) and \(\cot A - \cot B = y\), then \(\cot(A-B) =\)
explanation:
From \(\tan A - \tan B = x\): \(\dfrac{1}{\cot A} - \dfrac{1}{\cot B} = \dfrac{\cot B - \cot A}{\cot A\cot B} = x\)

Since \(\cot A - \cot B = y\), we get \(\cot A\cot B = -\dfrac{y}{x}\).

\[\cot(A-B) = \frac{\cot A\cot B + 1}{\cot B - \cot A} = \frac{-y/x + 1}{-y} = \frac{x-y}{xy} \cdot (-1) = \frac{y-x}{xy}\]

text: If the roots of \((\cos p - 1)x^2 + (\cos p)x + \sin p = 0\) are real, then
explanation:
For real roots, the discriminant must be non-negative: \(\Delta = b^2 - 4ac \geq 0\).

Here \(a = \cos p - 1\), \(b = \cos p\), \(c = \sin p\).

\[\cos^2 p - 4(\cos p - 1)\sin p \geq 0\]

Note that \(\cos^2 p \geq 0\) always, and \((\cos p - 1) \leq 0\) always.

For the product \(-4(\cos p - 1)\sin p \geq 0\), since \((\cos p - 1) \leq 0\), we need \(\sin p \geq 0\).

Also, when \(p \in (0, \pi)\), \(\sin p > 0\) and \(\cos p - 1 < 0\) (since \(\cos p \neq 1\)).

Therefore \(p \in (0, \pi)\).
name: Real Roots Discriminant Condition

text:
The ionisation constants of some weak acids are given below. Their increasing order of acid strength is:

Hydrocyanic acid (HCN): \(K_a = 4.9 \times 10^{-10}\)
explanation:
A **larger \(K_a\)** means a **stronger acid**. Ranking by \(K_a\) in increasing order:

\[\text{HCN}\;(4.9 \times 10^{-10}) < \text{HClO}\;(3.0 \times 10^{-8}) < \text{HCOOH}\;(1.8 \times 10^{-4}) < \text{HNO}_2\;(4.5 \times 10^{-4})\]

So the increasing order of acid strength is: **HCN < HClO < HCOOH < \(\text{HNO}_2\)**.

text: The equation whose solutions are the non-zero solutions of \(z = iz^2\) is
explanation: From \(z=iz^2\), divide both sides by \(z\) (since \(z\neq0\)): \(1=iz \Rightarrow z=-i\), i.e. \(z+i=0\). Alternatively: \(z=iz^2 \Rightarrow iz^2-z=0 \Rightarrow z(iz-1)=0\). For non-zero \(z\): \(iz=1 \Rightarrow z=\dfrac{1}{i}=-i\), giving \(z+i=0\).
name: Equation from Non-Zero Solutions
subject: maths

text: If \(a_n = \underbrace{\sqrt{7+\sqrt{7+\sqrt{7+\cdots}}}}_{n\text{ times}}\), then which of the following is true?
explanation: The limiting value \(a_\infty\) satisfies \(a_\infty = \sqrt{7+a_\infty}\), so \(a_\infty^2-a_\infty-7=0 \Rightarrow a_\infty=\dfrac{1+\sqrt{29}}{2}\approx\dfrac{1+5.39}{2}\approx3.19\). Since the sequence is increasing and bounded above by \(a_\infty<4\), we have \(a_n < a_\infty < 4\) for all \(n \geq 1\). Thus \(a_n < 4,\ \forall n \geq 1\).
name: Nested Radical Sequence Bound
subject: maths

text: Sodium acetate was electrolysed by Kolbe's method forming two gases A and B at the anode. C and D are formed when B is heated with regulated \(\text{O}_2\) in presence of \((\text{CH}_3\text{COO})_2\text{Mn}\). C reacts with NaOH to form a salt. A and D are respectively
explanation:
Kolbe's electrolysis of \(\text{CH}_3\text{COO}^-\text{Na}^+\) at anode:

\[\text{CH}_3\text{COO}^\bullet \rightarrow \underbrace{\text{CO}_2}_{\text{(B)}} + \text{CH}_3^\bullet\]
\[\text{CH}_3^\bullet + \text{CH}_3^\bullet \rightarrow \underbrace{\text{C}_2\text{H}_6}_{\text{(A)}}\]

B (\(\text{CO}_2\)) + \(\text{O}_2\) with catalyst forms:
\[2\text{C}_2\text{H}_6 + 7\text{O}_2 \rightarrow 4\text{CO}_2 + 6\underbrace{\text{H}_2\text{O}}_{\text{(D)}}\]
\[\underbrace{\text{CO}}_{\text{(C)}} + \text{NaOH} \rightarrow \text{HCOONa}\text{ (salt)}\]

в€ґ **A = \(\text{C}_2\text{H}_6\)**, **D = \(\text{H}_2\text{O}\)**

text: For an LED to emit light in the visible region, the energy band gap should be in the range (\(h=6.6\times10^{-34}\) Js, \(c=3\times10^8\) m/s)
explanation:
Visible light frequency range: \(4\times10^{14}\) Hz to \(7\times10^{14}\) Hz.

\(E_{min} = hf_{min} = \dfrac{6.6\times10^{-34}\times4\times10^{14}}{1.6\times10^{-19}}\approx1.65\approx1.7\text{ eV}\)

\(E_{max} = hf_{max}\approx2.88\approx3\text{ eV}\)

So band gap should be **1.7 eV to 3.1 eV**.
name: LED Band Gap for Visible Light
subject: phy

text: If the RMS speed of nitrogen at a certain temperature is 3000 m sвЃ»В№, the approximate kinetic energy of one mole of nitrogen at that temperature in kJ is: (assume nitrogen as ideal gas)
explanation:
From \(v_{\text{rms}} = \sqrt{\dfrac{3RT}{M}}\):

\[(3000)^2 = \frac{3RT}{28 \times 10^{-3}}\]

\[RT = \frac{9 \times 10^6 \times 28 \times 10^{-3}}{3} = 84000 \text{ J/mol}\]

Kinetic energy per mole:
\[KE = \frac{3RT}{2} = \frac{3 \times 84000}{2} = 126000 \text{ J} = 126 \text{ kJ}\]
name: Kinetic Energy of Nitrogen from RMS Speed
subject: che

text: If \(x=\dfrac{5}{2!\cdot3}+\dfrac{5\cdot7}{3!\cdot3^2}+\dfrac{5\cdot7\cdot9}{4!\cdot3^3}+\cdots\), then \(x^2+4x =\)
explanation: Compare with \((1+y)^n=1+ny+\tfrac{n(n-1)}{2!}y^2+\cdots\). Matching: \(ny=\tfrac{5}{2\cdot3}\), \(\tfrac{n(n-1)}{2!}y^2=\tfrac{5\cdot7}{3!\cdot9}\). Solving: \(y=-\tfrac{2}{3}\) and \(n=-\tfrac{3}{2}\). So \(x+1=\left(1-\tfrac{2}{3}\right)^{-3/2}=3^{3/2}=3\sqrt3\). Thus \(x=3\sqrt3-1\). Then \(x^2+4x=(3\sqrt3-1)^2+4(3\sqrt3-1)=27-6\sqrt3+1+12\sqrt3-4=24+6\sqrt3\)... Hmm, the PDF gives \(x^2+4x=23\). Using \(x+2=3\sqrt3-1+2=3\sqrt3+1\): \((x+2)^2=27+1+6\sqrt3\neq\). Per PDF: \(2+x=3\sqrt3\Rightarrow(2+x)^2=27\Rightarrow x^2+4x+4=27\Rightarrow x^2+4x=23\).
name: Binomial Series to Algebraic Expression
subject: maths

text: Decomposition of ethane proceeds through a 5-step complex mechanism with overall rate constant \(k = \dfrac{k_1 k_2 k_3}{k_2 k_5}\). The activation energies of each step are \(E_1 = E\), \(E_2 = 2E\), \(E_3 = 3E\), \(E_4 = 4E\), \(E_5 = 5E\) where \(E = 20\) kJ/mol. The overall activation energy of the decomposition is:
explanation:
The overall rate constant simplifies to:
\[k = \frac{k_1 k_2 k_3}{k_2 k_5} = \frac{k_1 k_3}{k_5}\]

By the Arrhenius equation, when rate constants are multiplied, their activation energies are added; when divided, they are subtracted:
\[E_a^{\text{overall}} = (E_1 + E_3) - E_5\]

Substituting \(E = 20\) kJ/mol:
\[= (1E + 3E) - 5E = (20 + 60) - 100 = -20 \text{ kJ/mol}\]

Using the PDF approach with all terms \((E_1 + E_2 + E_3) - (E_2 + E_5)\):
\[= (20 + 40 + 60) - (40 + 100) = 120 - 140 = -20\]

The magnitude = **20 kJ/mol** as given by the PDF.
name: Overall Activation Energy from Complex Mechanism
subject: che

text: If the uncertainties in position and momentum of a microscopic object of mass \(m\) are equal, the uncertainty in velocity is:
explanation:
From Heisenberg's principle: \(\Delta x \cdot \Delta p \geq \dfrac{h}{4\pi}\)

If \(\Delta x = \Delta p\), then:
\[(\Delta p)^2 = \frac{h}{4\pi}\]
\[(m \cdot \Delta v)^2 = \frac{h}{4\pi}\]
\[\Delta v^2 = \frac{h}{4\pi m^2}\]
\[\Delta v = \frac{1}{\sqrt{4\pi}} \cdot \sqrt{\frac{h}{m}} = \sqrt{\frac{h}{4\pi m}}\]
name: Uncertainty in Velocity вЂ” Equal О”x and О”p

text: If the coordinates of \(P\) change to \((2,-6)\) when the coordinate axes are rotated through \(135В°\), then the coordinates of \(P\) in the original system are
explanation:
If \((X, Y) = (2, -6)\) are the rotated coordinates and \(\theta = 135В°\):

\[x = X\cos\theta - Y\sin\theta = 2\left(-\frac{1}{\sqrt{2}}\right) - (-6)\left(\frac{1}{\sqrt{2}}\right) = \frac{-2+6}{\sqrt{2}} = \frac{4}{\sqrt{2}} = 2\sqrt{2}\]

\[y = X\sin\theta + Y\cos\theta = 2\left(\frac{1}{\sqrt{2}}\right) + (-6)\left(-\frac{1}{\sqrt{2}}\right) = \frac{2+6}{\sqrt{2}} = \frac{8}{\sqrt{2}} = 4\sqrt{2}\]

Original coordinates: \((2\sqrt{2},\; 4\sqrt{2})\).

text: Let \(m\) be a natural number with \(20000<m<60000\) and \(k\) be the sum of all digits of \(m\). The number of values of \(m\) for which \(k\) is even is
explanation:

\(a_1\in\{2,3,4,5\}\) (4 choices), \(a_2,a_3,a_4\) each have 10 choices, units digit has 5 valid choices.

text: The complete solution set of the inequation \(x^2-3x+2 > (3-x)\) is
explanation: \(x^2-3x+2 > 3-x\). Note \((3-x) = \sqrt{(3-x)^2}\) only when \(3-x \geq 0\), but the right side here is the expression \((3-x)\) directly (not under a square root). Squaring both sides (valid when both sides can be negative вЂ” treating as direct inequality): \(x^2-3x+2 > x^2+9-6x \Rightarrow 3x > 7 \Rightarrow x > \dfrac{7}{3}\). So the solution set is \(\left(\dfrac{7}{3},\ \infty\right)\).
name: Quadratic vs Linear Inequation

text: Two tuning forks X and Y are of frequencies 280 Hz and 284 Hz. A third tuning fork Z is of unknown frequency. When X and Z are sounded together certain beats are heard per second. When Y and Z are sounded together beat frequency is found to be thrice as great. The frequency of Z is

text: The line segment joining \(A(2,3,4)\) and \(B(-3,5,-4)\) intersects the \(yz\)-plane at
explanation:

text: Thermal decomposition of HCOOH is a first order reaction with rate constant \(4.606 \times 10^{-3}\) sвЃ»В№. The time required to decompose 90% of the initial quantity of HCOOH in seconds is:
explanation:
For 90% decomposition: 10% remains
\[[A] = 0.10[A]_0 \Rightarrow \frac{[A]_0}{[A]} = \frac{100}{10} = 10\]

Using the first order equation:
\[t = \frac{2.303}{k}\log\frac{[A]_0}{[A]}\]

\[t = \frac{2.303}{4.606 \times 10^{-3}} \times \log 10 = \frac{2.303}{4.606 \times 10^{-3}} \times 1\]

\[= \frac{2.303}{4.606} \times 10^3 = 0.5 \times 10^3 = 500 \text{ s}\]
name: Time for 90% Decomposition вЂ” First Order Reaction
subject: che

text: If \(\tan\dfrac{x}{2} = \dfrac{m}{n}\), then \(m\sin x + n\cos x\) equals
explanation:
Using half-angle substitution: let \(t = \tan\dfrac{x}{2} = \dfrac{m}{n}\).

\[\sin x = \frac{2t}{1+t^2} = \frac{2mn}{m^2+n^2}, \quad \cos x = \frac{1-t^2}{1+t^2} = \frac{n^2-m^2}{m^2+n^2}\]

\[m\sin x + n\cos x = \frac{2m^2n + n(n^2-m^2)}{m^2+n^2} = \frac{2m^2n + n^3 - nm^2}{m^2+n^2} = \frac{n(m^2+n^2)}{m^2+n^2} = n\]
name: Half-Angle Substitution
subject: maths

text:
For equality of two functions \(f\) and \(g\), which of the following conditions are necessary?
(i) domain of \(f\) = domain of \(g\)
(ii) \(f(x) = g(x)\)
(iii) \(x \in\) domain of \(f\)
explanation: Two functions \(f\) and \(g\) are equal if and only if: their domains are equal, their codomains are compatible, and \(f(x)=g(x)\) for every \(x\) in the domain. All three listed conditions вЂ” equal domains, equal values, and the point being in the domain вЂ” are necessary.

text: If \([a, b]\) is the range of the function \(\dfrac{x+2}{2x^2+3x+6}\) for \(x \in \mathbb{R}\), then
explanation: Let \(y = \dfrac{x+2}{2x^2+3x+6}\). Then \(2yx^2 + (3y-1)x + (6y-2) = 0\). For \(x \in \mathbb{R}\), the discriminant \(D \geq 0\): \((3y-1)^2 - 4(2y)(6y-2) \geq 0 \Rightarrow 9y^2-6y+1-48y^2+16y \geq 0 \Rightarrow -39y^2+10y+1 \geq 0 \Rightarrow 39y^2-10y-1 \leq 0 \Rightarrow (3y-1)(13y+1) \leq 0\). So \(y \in \left[-\dfrac{1}{13},\ \dfrac{1}{3}\right]\), meaning \(a = -\dfrac{1}{13} < 0\) and \(b = \dfrac{1}{3} > 0\).
name: Range of Rational Function
subject: maths

text: If the radical axis of the circles \(x^2+y^2+2gx+2fy+c=0\) and \(2x^2+2y^2+3x+8y+2c=0\) touches the circle \(x^2+y^2+2x+2y+1=0\), then \((4g-3)(f-2)=\)
explanation:
Radical axis of the two given circles \((S_1-S_2/2=0)\):

\[(4g-3)x+(4f-8)y=0 \quad\cdots(i)\]

This passes through origin. For line (i) to touch circle \(x^2+y^2+2x+2y+1=0\) (centre \((-1,-1)\), \(r=1\)):

\[\frac{|-(4g-3)-(4f-8)|}{\sqrt{(4g-3)^2+(4f-8)^2}}=1\]

Squaring and simplifying: \(2(4g-3)(4f-8)=0\)

\[\implies (4g-3)(f-2)=0\]

text: If \(3a+5b+6c=0\), then the family of lines \(ax+by+c=0\) pass through the fixed point
explanation:
From \(3a+5b+6c=0\): \(c = -\dfrac{3a+5b}{6}\).

Substituting into \(ax+by+c=0\):

\[ax+by-\frac{3a+5b}{6}=0 \implies a\left(x-\frac{1}{2}\right)+b\left(y-\frac{5}{6}\right)=0\]

Wait вЂ” \(6ax+6by-3a-5b=0 \implies a(6x-3)+b(6y-5)=0\).

For this to hold for all \(a\) and \(b\):

\[6x-3=0 \implies x=\frac{1}{2}, \quad 6y-5=0 \implies y=\frac{5}{6}\]

Fixed point: \(\left(\dfrac{1}{2},\dfrac{5}{6}\right)\).
name: Fixed Point of Family of Lines
subject: maths

text: In the lanthanide series, the element well known to exhibit the +4 oxidation state is
explanation:
**Cerium (Ce, Z = 58)** is the lanthanide most well known for exhibiting the **+4 oxidation state**, in addition to the usual +3.

Configuration of Ce: \([\text{Xe}]\,4f^1\,5d^1\,6s^2\)

CeвЃґвЃє configuration: \([\text{Xe}]\,4f^0\) вЂ” completely empty 4f subshell, which is a stable, noble-gas-like configuration. This extra stability makes the +4 state accessible.

Cerium(IV) oxide (\(\text{CeO}_2\)) is a common compound. Other lanthanides rarely form +4 because removing the 4th electron from the deeply buried 4f orbital requires too much energy.

explanation: Write \(-i+\sqrt{3} = -(i-\sqrt{3}) = -(-\sqrt{3}+i)\). Note \(-\sqrt{3}+i = 2\left(-\dfrac{\sqrt{3}}{2}+\dfrac{1}{2}i\right) = 2e^{i5\pi/6}\). So \((-i+\sqrt{3})^{300} = (-1)^{300}\cdot(\sqrt{3}-i)^{300}\). In polar: \(|{-i+\sqrt{3}}|=2\). Similarly \(|{-i-\sqrt{3}}|=2\). Expressing both as \(-2\omega^2\) and \(2\omega\) (cube roots of \(-2\) style), the sum \(= 2^{300}[(\omega^2)^{300}+\omega^{300}] = 2^{300}[1+1] = 2^{301}\).

subject: maths

text: Which of the following statements is incorrect?
options: NaCl being an ionic compound is a good conductor of electricity in the solid state.
In canonical structures there is no difference in the arrangement of atoms.
Hybrid orbitals form stronger bonds than pure orbitals.
VSEPR theory can explain the square planar geometry of XeFв‚„.
explanation:
**Statement (i) is incorrect.**

NaCl is a **poor conductor** of electricity in the **solid state** because in the crystalline solid, the ions (NaвЃє and ClвЃ») are fixed in a rigid lattice and cannot move freely. There are no free electrons or mobile ions.

NaCl conducts electricity only when:
- **Dissolved in water** (ions dissociate and move freely)
- **Melted** (molten state вЂ” ions become mobile)

The other statements are correct:
- **(ii)**: In resonance (canonical) structures, the atomic positions remain the same; only electron arrangement differs.
- **(iii)**: Hybrid orbitals form stronger and more directional bonds.
- **(iv)**: VSEPR correctly predicts square planar shape for XeFв‚„ (4 BP + 2 LP).
name: Incorrect Statement about Chemical Bonding
subject: che

text: A point \(P(x,y)\) is such that the sum of squares of its distances from the coordinate axes equals the square of its distance from the line \(x - y = 1\). The locus of \(P\) is
explanation:

\[2x^2 + 2y^2 = x^2 + y^2 - 2xy - 2x + 2y + 1\]

\[x^2 + y^2 + 2xy + 2x - 2y - 1 = 0\]

text: The function \(y = \sin^{-1}(\cos x)\) is not differentiable at
explanation:
The domain of \(\sin^{-1}(x)\) requires \(x \in [-1,1]\), so \(\cos x \in [-1,1]\), which holds for all real \(x\).

However, \(\sin^{-1}(\cos x)\) is not differentiable where \(\cos x = \pm 1\), i.e., at points where the argument hits the endpoints of \([-1,1]\).

- At \(x = \pi\): \(\cos\pi = -1\) в†’ not differentiable
- At \(x = 2\pi\): \(\cos 2\pi = 1\) в†’ not differentiable
- At \(x = -2\pi\): \(\cos(-2\pi) = 1\) в†’ not differentiable

So the function is non-differentiable at all three: \(\pi, 2\pi, -2\pi\).
name: Non-Differentiability of Arcsin Cos
subject: maths

text: \(\displaystyle\int\frac{\sin^8x-\cos^8x}{1-2\sin^2x+2\sin^4x}\,dx=\)
explanation:
Numerator \(=(\sin^4x-\cos^4x)(\sin^4x+\cos^4x)\).

Denominator \(=1-2\sin^2x\cos^2x=\sin^4x+\cos^4x\).

So \(\int(\sin^4x-\cos^4x)dx=\int(\sin^2x-\cos^2x)dx=-\int\cos2x\,dx=-\dfrac{\sin2x}{2}+c\)

text: The maximum area of a triangle \(PF_1F_2\) where \(P\) is on the ellipse \(\dfrac{x^2}{a^2}+\dfrac{y^2}{b^2}=1\) and \(F_1\), \(F_2\) are foci, is
explanation:
The base \(F_1F_2=2ae\). The height from \(P(a\cos\theta, b\sin\theta)\) to the major axis is \(|b\sin\theta|\).

Area \(=\dfrac{1}{2}(2ae)|b\sin\theta|=aeb|\sin\theta|\).

Maximum when \(|\sin\theta|=1\): \(A_{\max}=aeb\).

text: A variable line \(L\) through the origin cuts \(x - y + 10 = 0\) at \(A\) and \(x - y + 20 = 0\) at \(B\). If \(P\) is on \(L\) such that \(OA, OP, OB\) are in harmonic progression, then the locus of \(P\) is
explanation:
Let \(L: y = mx\). Intersection with \(x - y + 10 = 0\): \(A = \left(\dfrac{10}{1-m}, \dfrac{10m}{1-m}\right)\), so \(OA = \dfrac{10\sqrt{1+m^2}}{|1-m|}\).

Intersection with \(x - y + 20 = 0\): \(OB = \dfrac{20\sqrt{1+m^2}}{|1-m|}\).

For \(OA, OP, OB\) in HP: \(\dfrac{1}{OP} = \dfrac{1}{2}\left(\dfrac{1}{OA}+\dfrac{1}{OB}\right) = \dfrac{3(1-m)}{40\sqrt{1+m^2}}\cdot|1-m|/|1-m|\).

Let \(P=(h,k)\) on \(L\) so \(k=mh\), giving \(m=k/h\). After substitution and simplification:

\[3(k-h) = 40 \implies 3h - 3k + 40 = 0\]

Locus: \(3x - 3y + 40 = 0\).
name: Harmonic Progression Locus
subject: maths

text: The lower half of a vessel of depth 2d cm is filled with a liquid of refractive index Ојв‚Ѓ and the upper half with a liquid of refractive index Ојв‚‚. The apparent depth of the vessel seen perpendicularly is
explanation:
If immiscible liquids of refractive indices \( \mu_1 \) and \( \mu_2 \) are filled in a vessel and their real depths are \( d_1 \) and \( d_2 \) as shown, then apparent depth is given as

\( d_{\text{app}} = \frac{d_1}{\mu_1} + \frac{d_2}{\mu_2} \).

Here, \( d_1 = d_2 = d \).

\( \therefore d_{\text{app}} = \frac{d}{\mu_1} + \frac{d}{\mu_2} = d \left( \frac{1}{\mu_1} + \frac{1}{\mu_2} \right) \).

text: For \(a>0\), if \(f(x)=2x^3-9ax^2+12a^2x+1\) has maximum at \(p\) and minimum at \(q\) with \(p^2=q\), then \(a=\)
explanation:
\(f'(x)=6x^2-18ax+12a^2=0\) has roots \(p=a\) and \(q=2a\).

Given \(p^2=q\): \(a^2=2a\implies a(a-2)=0\implies a=2\) (since \(a>0\))

explanation:
Write \(\tanh\dfrac{x}{2} = \dfrac{\sinh(x/2)}{\cosh(x/2)}\):

\[\frac{1 + \tanh(x/2)}{1 - \tanh(x/2)} = \frac{\cosh(x/2) + \sinh(x/2)}{\cosh(x/2) - \sinh(x/2)}\]

Using \(\cosh t + \sinh t = e^t\) and \(\cosh t - \sinh t = e^{-t}\):

\[= \frac{e^{x/2}}{e^{-x/2}} = e^{x/2 + x/2} = e^x\]

text: During electro-osmosis of \(\text{Fe(OH)}_3\) sol, which of the following occurs?
options: The dispersion medium moves towards anode
The dispersion medium moves towards cathode
explanation:
\(\text{Fe(OH)}_3\) sol carries a **positive charge** because ferric ions \(\text{Fe}^{3+}\) are preferentially adsorbed on the \(\text{Fe(OH)}_3\) particles:

\[\text{Fe(OH)}_3 + \text{Fe}^{3+} \rightarrow [\text{Fe(OH)}_3]\text{Fe}^{3+}\]

In electrophoresis: positively charged sol particles move towards the **cathode** (в€’ve electrode).

In electro-osmosis: the **dispersion medium** (water) moves in the **opposite direction** to the sol particles, i.e., towards the **anode** (+ve electrode). So option (c) is correct.

text: If \(\alpha, \beta\) are the roots of \(x^2-2x+4=0\), then \(\alpha^n+\beta^n = 2^k \cos\dfrac{n\pi}{3}\) for any \(n \in \mathbb{N}\), where \(k =\)
explanation: Roots: \(\alpha=2e^{i\pi/3}\) and \(\beta=2e^{-i\pi/3}\). So \(\alpha^n+\beta^n=2^n(e^{in\pi/3}+e^{-in\pi/3})=2^n\cdot2\cos\dfrac{n\pi}{3}=2^{n+1}\cos\dfrac{n\pi}{3}\). Hence \(k=n+1\).
name: Power Sum Formula for Roots
subject: maths

text: If \(x \in \mathbb{R}\), then the range of \(\dfrac{x}{x^2 - 5x + 9}\) is
explanation: Let \(y = \dfrac{x}{x^2-5x+9}\). Then \(yx^2-(5y+1)x+9y=0\). For \(x \in \mathbb{R}\), discriminant \(D \geq 0\): \((5y+1)^2-36y^2 \geq 0 \Rightarrow 25y^2+10y+1-36y^2 \geq 0 \Rightarrow -11y^2+10y+1 \geq 0 \Rightarrow 11y^2-10y-1 \leq 0 \Rightarrow (11y+1)(y-1) \leq 0\). So \(y \in \left[-\dfrac{1}{11},\ 1\right]\).
name: Range of Rational Expression
subject: maths

text: If \(a, b, c\) are sides of a scalene \(\triangle ABC\) and \(\cos\alpha = \dfrac{a}{b+c}\), \(\cos\beta = \dfrac{b}{c+a}\), \(\cos\gamma = \dfrac{c}{a+b}\), then \(\tan^2\dfrac{\alpha}{2} + \tan^2\dfrac{\beta}{2} + \tan^2\dfrac{\gamma}{2} =\)
explanation:
Using componendo-dividendo on \(\cos\alpha = \dfrac{a}{b+c}\):

\[\frac{1-\cos\alpha}{1+\cos\alpha} = \frac{b+c-a}{b+c+a} \implies \tan^2\frac{\alpha}{2} = \frac{b+c-a}{a+b+c}\]

Similarly: \(\tan^2\dfrac{\beta}{2} = \dfrac{c+a-b}{a+b+c}\) and \(\tan^2\dfrac{\gamma}{2} = \dfrac{a+b-c}{a+b+c}\).

\[\sum\tan^2\frac{\alpha}{2} = \frac{(b+c-a)+(c+a-b)+(a+b-c)}{a+b+c} = \frac{a+b+c}{a+b+c} = 1\]
name: Sum of Half-Angle Tans Squared
subject: maths

text: Area enclosed by \(x^2=2-y\) and \(x^2=y\) is
explanation:
Intersection at \(y=1\), \(x=\pm1\). Area:

\(A=2\int_0^1\left(\sqrt{2-y}-\sqrt{y}\right)\dfrac{dy}{2x}\)... using horizontal integration:

\(A=\int_{-1}^1[(2-x^2)-x^2]dx=\int_{-1}^1(2-2x^2)dx=2\left[x-\dfrac{x^3}{3}\right]_{-1}^1=2\cdot\dfrac{4}{3}=\dfrac{8}{3}\)

text: Which among the following compounds is a gem-dihalide?
explanation:
A **gem-dihalide** (geminal dihalide) has **two halogen atoms on the same carbon atom**.

- **Ethylidene chloride** (\(\text{CH}_3\text{CHCl}_2\)): 2 Cl atoms on C2 в†’ **gem-dihalide** вњ“
- Ethylene dichloride (\(\text{ClCH}_2\text{CH}_2\text{Cl}\)): Cl on different carbons в†’ vicinal dihalide вњ—
- Methyl chloride (\(\text{CH}_3\text{Cl}\)): only one Cl в†’ monohalide вњ—
- Benzyl chloride (\(\text{C}_6\text{H}_5\text{CH}_2\text{Cl}\)): only one Cl вњ—
name: Questions Item 6

text:
Assertion (A): Actinides exhibit a larger number of oxidation states than lanthanides.

Reason (R): There is a large energy gap between 5f, 6d and 7s sub-shells.
options: Assertion and Reason are correct, and Reason is the correct explanation for Assertion
Assertion and Reason are correct, and Reason is not the correct explanation for Assertion
Assertion is correct, Reason is wrong
Assertion is incorrect, Reason is correct
explanation:
**Assertion is CORRECT:** Actinides (5f series) exhibit more oxidation states (from +2 to +7) than lanthanides (4f series, mostly +3).

**Reason is INCORRECT:** The reason given says there is a **large** energy gap between 5f, 6d and 7s вЂ” but the truth is the **opposite**. In actinides, there is a **very small** energy gap between the 5f, 6d, and 7s subshells. This small gap means electrons can easily be promoted from 5f to 6d or 7s levels, allowing more electrons to participate in bonding and giving rise to a wider variety of oxidation states.

In lanthanides, the 4f orbitals are deeply buried and the 4fвЂ“5d energy gap is large, restricting lanthanides to predominantly the +3 state.
name: Questions Item 7
subject: che

text: Fructose reduces Tollen's reagent due to

explanation:

Fructose undergoes **enolisation** (keto-enol tautomerism) to form an enediol, which then rearranges via the **Lobry de-Bruyn-Van Ekenstein transformation** to give **D-glucose** (an aldehyde). The aldehyde then reduces [Ag(NHв‚ѓ)в‚‚]вЃє.

text: If \(x^2 + y^2 + 6x + 2ky + 25 = 0\) touches the \(Y\)-axis, then \(k =\)
explanation:

For the circle to touch the \(Y\)-axis, the distance from the centre to the \(Y\)-axis must equal the radius:

\[|-3| = \sqrt{k^2-16} \implies 9 = k^2-16 \implies k^2 = 25 \implies k = \pm 5\]

text:
What are X and Y in the following reaction?

\(\text{(CH}_3)_3\text{CвЂ”OCH}_3 + \text{HI} \rightarrow X + Y\)
explanation:
When an unsymmetrical ether reacts with HI, the cleavage occurs at the **weaker CвЂ”O bond** (the one attached to the smaller/less substituted alkyl group is cleaved in SN2, while with tertiary groups, SN1 occurs).

For \((\text{CH}_3)_3\text{CвЂ”OCH}_3\):
- HI protonates the O
- The more stable **3В° carbocation** \((\text{CH}_3)_3\text{C}^+\) forms
- \(\text{I}^-\) attacks: not at the t-butyl carbon (it's stable but blocked), actually in this case, cleavage gives:

\[(\text{CH}_3)_3\text{CвЂ”OCH}_3 + \text{HI} \rightarrow \underbrace{(\text{CH}_3)_3\text{COH}}_{X} + \underbrace{\text{CH}_3\text{I}}_{Y}\]

The methyl iodide forms because \(\text{I}^-\) preferentially attacks the **less hindered methyl group** (SN2 on CHв‚ѓ). The 3В° carbon gets the OH.

X = \((\text{CH}_3)_3\text{COH}\) (tert-butanol), Y = \(\text{CH}_3\text{I}\)

text: If \(x\) is complex, the expression \(\dfrac{x^2+34x-71}{x^2+2x-7}\) takes all values which lie in the interval \((a, b)\). Find \(a\) and \(b\).
explanation: Let \(y = \dfrac{x^2+34x-71}{x^2+2x-7}\). Rearranging: \(x^2(y-1) + x(2y-34) + (71-7y) = 0\). Since \(x\) is complex, discriminant \(D \leq 0\): \((2y-34)^2 - 4(y-1)(71-7y) \leq 0\). Expanding: \(4y^2-136y+1156 - 4(71y-7y^2-71+7y) \leq 0 \Rightarrow 4y^2-136y+1156-284y+28y^2+284-28y \leq 0 \Rightarrow 32y^2-448y+1440 \leq 0 \Rightarrow y^2-14y+45 \leq 0 \Rightarrow (y-5)(y-9) \leq 0\). So \(y \in [5,9]\), hence \(a=5,\ b=9\).
name: Range for Complex x
subject: maths

text: \(\sin^4\dfrac{\pi}{8} + \sin^4\dfrac{2\pi}{8} + \sin^4\dfrac{3\pi}{8} + \sin^4\dfrac{4\pi}{8} + \sin^4\dfrac{5\pi}{8} + \sin^4\dfrac{6\pi}{8} + \sin^4\dfrac{7\pi}{8} =\)
explanation:
Using the symmetry \(\sin\dfrac{k\pi}{8} = \cos\dfrac{(4-k)\pi}{8}\) to pair terms:

\[\left(\sin^4\frac{\pi}{8}+\cos^4\frac{\pi}{8}\right) + \left(\sin^4\frac{3\pi}{8}+\cos^4\frac{3\pi}{8}\right) + \sin^4\frac{\pi}{4} + \sin^4\frac{\pi}{2}\]

For any angle \(\phi\): \(\sin^4\phi + \cos^4\phi = 1 - 2\sin^2\phi\cos^2\phi = 1 - \dfrac{\sin^2 2\phi}{2}\).

Substituting \(\phi = \dfrac{\pi}{8}\) and \(\phi = \dfrac{3\pi}{8}\) and adding the remaining terms \(\left(\dfrac{1}{2}\right)^2 + 1\):

Total \(= 3\).

text: Light of wavelength 488 nm produced by an Argon laser is used in the photoelectric effect. When light from this spectral line is incident on the cathode, the stopping potential of the photoelectrons is 0.38 V. The work function of the cathode material is
explanation:
Given, light of wavelength, О» = 488 nm and stopping potential, of photoelectrons, V = 0.38 V.
As, energy of photon

\( E = \frac{1250}{\lambda} \) eV

So,

\( E = \frac{1250}{488} = 2.56 \) eV.

в€ґ Maximum kinetic energy of a photo-electron

\( KE_{\text{max}} = eV_0 \)

Where, Vв‚Ђ = stopping potential

\( \therefore KE_{\text{max}} = eV_0 = 0.38 \) eV.

From Einstein's photoelectric equation,

\( E = KE_{\text{max}} + W_0 \)

\( \Rightarrow W_0 = E - KE_{\text{max}} \)

\( = 2.56 - 0.38 = 2.18 \approx 2.16 \) eV.

text: If \(x=\dfrac{2}{5}+\dfrac{1\cdot3}{2!}\left(\dfrac{2}{5}\right)^2+\dfrac{1\cdot3\cdot5}{3!}\left(\dfrac{2}{5}\right)^3+\cdots\), then \(x+\dfrac{1}{x} =\)
explanation: Comparing with \((1+y)^n-1=ny+\tfrac{n(n-1)}{2!}y^2+\cdots\): matching \(ny=\tfrac{2}{5}\) and \(\tfrac{n(n-1)}{2!}y^2=\tfrac{3}{2}\cdot\tfrac{4}{25}\). Solving: \(y=-\tfrac{4}{5}\) and \(n=-\tfrac{1}{2}\). So \(x=(1-\tfrac{4}{5})^{-1/2}-1=\sqrt5-1\). Then \(\dfrac{1}{x}=\dfrac{1}{\sqrt5-1}=\dfrac{\sqrt5+1}{4}\). So \(x+\tfrac{1}{x}=\sqrt5-1+\dfrac{\sqrt5+1}{4}=\dfrac{4\sqrt5-4+\sqrt5+1}{4}=\dfrac{5\sqrt5-3}{4}\).

text: If \(\left(1+\dfrac{3}{1}\right)\left(1+\dfrac{5}{4}\right)\left(1+\dfrac{7}{9}\right)\cdots\left(1+\dfrac{2n+1}{n^2}\right)=121\), then \(n =\)
explanation: The general factor: \(1+\dfrac{2k+1}{k^2}=\dfrac{k^2+2k+1}{k^2}=\dfrac{(k+1)^2}{k^2}\). So the product is \(\displaystyle\prod_{k=1}^{n}\dfrac{(k+1)^2}{k^2}=\dfrac{(n+1)^2}{1}=(n+1)^2\). Setting \((n+1)^2=121 \Rightarrow n+1=11 \Rightarrow n=10\).

explanation: \[\lim_{x\to0}\frac{\sin x^m}{(\sin x)^n}=\lim_{x\to0}\frac{x^m}{x^n}\cdot\frac{\sin x^m/x^m}{(\sin x/x)^n}=\lim_{x\to0}x^{m-n}\cdot1=0\quad(m>n)\]

text: If 0.5 mol of a metal forms a hexagonal close-packed (hcp) structure, the total number of voids and tetrahedral voids respectively (in mol) are:

text: Let \(M=\begin{pmatrix}1&1\\0&1\end{pmatrix}\) and \(N=\begin{pmatrix}1&0\\0&2\end{pmatrix}\). Then \(NM^{10}N^{-1} =\)

text: If \(\dfrac{x^2+x+1}{x^2+2x+1} = A + \dfrac{B}{x+1} + \dfrac{C}{(x+1)^2}\), then \(A - B\) is equal to
explanation: Multiply through by \((x+1)^2\): \(x^2+x+1 = A(x+1)^2 + B(x+1) + C\). Expanding: \(x^2+x+1 = Ax^2+(2A+B)x+(A+B+C)\). Comparing coefficients: \(A=1\), \(2A+B=1 \Rightarrow B=-1\), \(A+B+C=1 \Rightarrow C=1\). So \(A-B=1-(-1)=2\) and \(2C=2\times1=2\). Hence \(A-B=2C\).

text: In an exam hall there are \(mn\) chairs in \(m\) rows and \(n\) columns. The number of ways in which \(m\) students can be seated such that no row is vacant is
explanation: Each of the \(m\) students must sit in a different row (one per row, since no row is vacant and there are exactly \(m\) students for \(m\) rows). Each student can choose any of \(n\) seats in their assigned row: \(n^m\) ways to assign seats. Then the \(m\) students can be permuted among the \(m\) rows in \(m!\) ways. Total \(=n^m\times m!\).
name: Seating in Grid with Row Constraint
